name: OData entity collection enumerable without authentication
description: GETs an OData entity set — /odata/Orders — with no credentials and checks the response. When the service returns the full collection (an OData JSON envelope with a value array of entities) to an unauthenticated caller, the entire data set is enumerable by anyone: excessive data exposure plus broken function-level authorization on the collection endpoint. Unlike the single-object BOLA check (bwr-odata-002), this is bulk enumeration — one anonymous request pulls every row, and the records span multiple owners, so it is clearly not a per-caller-scoped view. The predicate requires the OData collection envelope AND more than one owner value, so a correctly-scoped single-tenant response doesn't trip it.

vulnerability.remediation: Require authentication + authorization on every OData entity set, and scope the returned query to the caller's own records — never return the full collection. ASP.NET Core OData: gate the route with `.RequireAuthorization()` and apply a per-caller server-side filter (`.Where(o => o.OwnerId == currentUserId)`) before the OData query options run; cap `$top` with `[EnableQuery(MaxTop = …)]` so an authorized caller still can't pull unbounded data. Verify with `curl -k https://host/odata/Orders` — an authenticated, scoped endpoint returns 401 anonymously and only the caller's own rows once authorized.
